feat(llm-analyzer): Ollama REST client for ad transcript analysis (#17)

* feat(llm-analyzer): Ollama REST client with structured ad analysis

Add src/llm_analyzer.py — a stdlib-only (urllib) Ollama REST client that
sends an ad transcript to a local LLM and returns structured fields:
product_name, ad_type, summary, tone.

- analyze_transcript(): POST to /api/generate, timeout=120s
- _parse_response(): strips Markdown fences, JSON-parses, graceful
  fallback to raw_response when LLM returns non-JSON
- LlmAnalysisResult: @final __slots__ class with @override __repr__

Config:
- src/config.py: add OLLAMA_MODEL env var (default: llama3.2)
- docker-compose.yml: expose OLLAMA_MODEL

Tests (14, coverage 100%):
- _parse_response: all fields, null/empty/fence/invalid-JSON cases
- analyze_transcript: happy path, URLError, model override, default model
- LlmAnalysisResult: repr with and without None fields

commit.message:
fix(docker): add build-essential for webrtcvad C extension (#16)

* fix(docker): add build-essential for webrtcvad C extension

webrtcvad==2.0.10 (via resemblyzer) requires gcc and libc headers.
python:3.12-slim ships without build tools; build-essential provides
both gcc and libc-dev so the C extension compiles successfully.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(docker): use multi-stage build to exclude build-essential from runtime

Build tools (build-essential) are needed only to compile C-extension
packages (webrtcvad via resemblyzer). Keeping them in the final image
increases size and attack surface unnecessarily.

Multi-stage approach:
- builder: installs build-essential, runs uv sync
- runtime: copies only .venv + src, no build tools

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: fallback through all LRC candidates on fetch failure

Previously, only the first synced candidate was tried. If its LRC file
was missing (404) or had invalid path chars like null bytes (400), the
entire repo was skipped.

Now iterates all candidates (synced first, preserving order) until one
succeeds. Skips paths containing null bytes. Only gives up when all
candidates fail.

Recovers ~24% of repos that were previously lost.

* Initial plan

* fix(security): prevent script injection in action.yml

- Pass `inputs.atmos-config-path` through env var and quote it in
  `realpath` call to prevent command injection
- Pass `inputs.base-ref` and `inputs.skip-checkout` through env vars
  and use a bash array in the `git checkout` call to prevent injection
- Remove the `affected-args` intermediate step that built a raw shell
  string from unquoted `${{ inputs.* }}` expressions

  bash arrays built from env vars, eliminating arbitrary code execution
  via user-controlled inputs (`atmos-stack`, `identity`, etc.)

affected command args' step that writes args (one per line) to
${RUNNER_TEMP}/atmos-affected-args. Both 'affected-pro' and 'affected'
steps then read them back with mapfile, passing only the step-specific
flag (--upload or --file=) inline. No eval, no injection surface.

commit.message:
feat: scan process resilience — phase-independent recovery with retry

Critical fix: Phase 1 timeout/failure no longer terminates all phases.

Architecture changes:
- Phase 1 (scan) wrapped in independent try-catch — failure shows
  warning and continues to Phase 2/3 with existing pending tasks
- Phase 2/3: per-task retry loop (2 attempts, 3s delay between)
- Server-unreachable detection with auto-reconnection wait (30s)
- scanInProgress lock prevents duplicate concurrent scans
- Each phase queries server-side task status independently:
  Phase 2 works on enrichmentStatus=pending regardless of Phase 1
  Phase 3 works on enrichmentStatus=enriched regardless of Phase 2
- Live frozen status shows retry progress on task cards

All 7 verification checks passed. CHANGELOG updated with v2.9.0.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add SystemVerilog testbenches for RTL primitives library

Create 16 standalone testbench files in testbenches/ directory covering:
- CDC: sync_2ff, async_fifo
- Arbiters: fixed_priority_arbiter, round_robin_arbiter

- Memory: dual_port_ram, simple_fifo

compilation unit.

All modules follow project conventions: snake_case signals, UPPER_CASE
parameters, always_ff/always_comb, active-low synchronous reset (rst_n),
no initial blocks, no vendor-specific primitives.

fix: kill existing server before restart in START-AGENT-ZERO.bat

Previously the launcher just opened the browser if port 3000 was busy,
leaving the old server running. Now it finds the PID on port 3000,
terminates it, waits 2 seconds, then starts a fresh server.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: intelligent duplicate detection with subject normalization and task reactivation

Problem: Scan created duplicate tasks for the same email thread when
subject prefixes differed (e.g. 'Re: RG Netzwerkprüfung' vs
'[EXTERN] RG Netzwerkprüfung'). Done tasks were only skipped, never
reactivated when new activity appeared.

Changes:
- Add stripSubjectPrefixes() to remove Re:, Fw:, AW:, WG:, [EXTERN],
  [EXTERNAL] etc. before comparison
- normalizeForCompare() now preserves German umlauts (äöüß) and strips
  prefixes before lowercasing
- isSimilarTitle() uses dual threshold: Jaccard >0.6 OR subset ratio
  >=0.9 (catches prefix-only differences)
- Safety-net now reactivates done tasks instead of creating duplicates:
  sets status to needs-attention, adds reactivation history entry,
  resets enrichment for fresh content extraction
- Backward-compat dedup uses normalized comparison instead of exact match
- AI scan prompt explicitly instructs to ignore subject prefixes
- Add session lifecycle management and orphan subprocess reaper

commit.message:
feat: auto-wrap flat SKILL.md repos as skills (#232) (#234)

When a plugin has no skills/ subdirectory, scan the plugin root for
directories containing SKILL.md and treat each as a skill. This gives
compatibility with the npx skills ecosystem (e.g. vercel-labs/agent-skills)
without any schema changes.

The enabledSkills/disabledSkills schema continues to work unchanged
since the plugin is still tracked as a workspace plugin entry.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: support installing skills individually (#228) (#231)

- Fix plugin install --skill when plugin already installed: detect
  'Plugin already exists' error and if --skill flags provided, skip
  the install step and proceed directly to enabling skills + sync

- Add top-level `allagents skills` command as shorthand alias for
  `allagents plugin skills` (list/add/remove), giving UX parity
  with `npx skills`

- Enhance `skills add` with --from/-f option: if skill not found in
  installed plugins, installs the specified plugin source first then
  enables the skill (mirrors `npx skills add <pkg> --skill <name>`)
